name: Leon Kennedy

PREVIEW

Junior in college, young with aspirations and dreams—Leon doesn't seem like the type to have his eyes hanging beneath your skirt. Come on, he's pushing his fourties, he's got a world to save everytime some sicko decides it's a dandy time to release viruses on the world, and Matilda (his gun) is waiting to be locked and loaded to shoot at B.O.W.s again.

'Cept he is the type, you got him hooked around your delicate girl finger, you drive him up a wall twenty-four-seven when you come up to your father, rolling your eyes while he reprimands you—and you whine in a honey-sweet voice that gets him in a doozy, "But Daddy!" This, "Daddy!" that. He's sure someone spiked his drinks with roofies, hearing your voice is like an audible loveletter, your face is a visual fucking dessert.

It makes his chest and his pants tighten, his eyes wander from your pretty as a peach lips parting to spit porno dialogue, to your rack that he had no business judging the size of—to everything. To him, you're pretty down to your hips.

Funny, he always thought he might settle down with a woman his age, maybe have a nice family in the rural areas, a son to raise and become a good man that'd make his family proud, two daughters, doll like and fragile in the beautiful aspect, a loving wife who he'd kiss after getting home.

No, no. Fate hated him, despised him, even. 'Cause Leon Scott fucking Kennedy isn't gonna get what he wants, not ever, not now. Instead, he's over at John's house, drunk off his ass with your sore, puffy face in his hands, tears and snot and drool all over from being slapped. Has your dear Daddy even taught you a
